description: Small benchmark issue set for validating ADLC applicability gating, verifier selection, and deterministic backtest policy routing.

cases.title: Fix sqlparser 0.43 compile break in SQL classifier
cases.summary: Build-validation task to restore cargo build/test truthfulness after a dependency API mismatch.

classification_evidence: Scope is limited to compile, test, lint, and CI truthfulness.
No new runtime behavior, endpoints, or integrations are introduced.

supported.claim: The repo does not currently pass cargo test.

supported_claims: Compile/test validation is the stated scope.

section_policy.reason: No new attack surface or auth change is introduced.
No runtime path or user-facing operation changes.
The task is defined by build and test verification.

primary_verifier.rationale: The known repo failure is already exposed by the command.

verification_spec.scope_note: Compile truthfulness is the primary contract for this task.

cases.expected_rationale: Layer 1 only mechanizes section defaults with direct change-surface signals. This case has no target_files or post_qa block, so verifier_scope is legacy_warn and test_strength is n_a.

cases.title: Clear clippy warnings in the workspace root crate
cases.summary: Lint cleanup task with no behavior change.

classification_evidence: The request is framed around clippy warnings, not new behavior.
No interface or runtime path change is required.

supported.claim: The crate should pass clippy with -D warnings.

supported_claims: Lint cleanup is the complete scope.

section_policy.reason: Lint cleanup does not add attack surface.
Lint cleanup does not change runtime paths.
The task succeeds only when static analysis passes.

primary_verifier.rationale: The lint failure is the direct pre-change signal.

verification_spec.scope_note: No broader test invention is needed for lint-only work.

cases.expected_rationale: No change-surface flags are active, so only core routing applies. target_files is unset and post_qa is absent, which yields legacy_warn plus n_a.

cases.title: Stop duplicate invoices on idempotent payment retries
cases.summary: Bugfix task with a non-sequitur claim that should be flagged before planning.

classification_evidence: The issue describes existing broken behavior under retry.
The expected fix is to reproduce and remove the duplicate side effect.

supported.claim: A retry currently creates duplicate invoices.

unsupported.claim: This is not the current scaffold JSONL logger with a new name.
unsupported.reason: The statement does not follow from the billing bug.

supported_claims: Duplicate invoices are the bug under repair.
unsupported_claims: This is not the current scaffold JSONL logger with a new name.
suspect_phrases: not the current scaffold JSONL logger with a new name

section_policy.reason: The fix does not introduce a new attack surface.
Invoice creation is a runtime path and user-facing billing operation.
The bug impacts billing integrity and retry safety.

primary_verifier.target: integration test: payment retry creates one invoice
primary_verifier.rationale: A reproducer is the shortest path to the existing failure.

verification_spec.scope_note: Bugfixes should preserve the failing reproducer through handoff.

cases.expected_rationale: The unsupported claim remains contamination metadata, but Layer 1 triage routing is confidence-band only. Runtime and user-facing flags activate observability and operator checks; target_files and post_qa are still absent.

cases.title: Add CSV export command for account activity
cases.summary: Feature task that adds a user-facing operation without new attack surface.

classification_evidence: The task adds net-new behavior.
The operation is user-facing and runtime-visible.

supported.claim: Users need a CSV export command for account activity.

supported_claims: CSV export is the desired new capability.

section_policy.reason: The feature does not change auth, attack surface, or external integrations.
The new export command is a runtime and user-facing operation.
The feature introduces a new interface and output format.

primary_verifier.target: acceptance test: CSV export emits expected headers and rows
primary_verifier.rationale: A behavior-defining test is the correct feature verifier.

secondary_verifiers.target: edge-case test: empty activity exports header-only CSV

verification_spec.scope_note: New features should be defined by explicit success cases and edge cases.

cases.expected_rationale: This layer drives observability from runtime and user-facing flags, and drives architecture-related overlays from api/data-format signals. Rollout is left not_applicable because the manifest does not expose a dedicated deployment trigger field.

cases.title: Add OAuth callback endpoint and encrypted token storage for Google Drive import
cases.summary: Security-sensitive feature with external integration, auth change, and persistent storage.

classification_evidence: The task adds a new auth flow and external integration.
The change stores third-party tokens and introduces a callback endpoint.

supported.claim: Add OAuth callback handling and token storage for Google Drive import.

supported_claims: OAuth callback and token storage are explicitly in scope.

section_policy.reason: The task adds auth, external integration, token handling, and a new endpoint.
The callback and import flow are runtime operations that need auditability.
The task changes interfaces, auth behavior, and dependency boundaries.

primary_verifier.target: acceptance test: OAuth callback stores encrypted token and starts import
primary_verifier.rationale: The feature is best defined through acceptance coverage of the auth flow.

secondary_verifiers.target: security test: callback rejects invalid state parameter

verification_spec.scope_note: Security-sensitive features require both primary behavior coverage and active overlay checks.

cases.expected_rationale: Security, runtime, and interface-related surfaces are all active here, so the deterministic layer activates the full security, observability, and integration overlays. target_files remains unset, so Gate 0 records a legacy warning instead of blocking.

cases.title: Investigate whether the flaky deploy script bug or docs drift is the real issue
cases.summary: Low-confidence intake that should not proceed automatically because the task classification signal is below threshold.

classification_evidence: The task mentions deploy-script behavior but does not pin down the failing path.
The issue mixes possible docs drift with a possible runtime automation bug.

supported.claim: There may be an issue in the deploy-script path.

needs_clarification: Whether the failure is actually in deploy automation or in stale documentation.

section_policy.reason: The uncertainty itself is a planning risk.

primary_verifier.target: deploy validation command to be confirmed by a human
primary_verifier.rationale: The likely path is infra, but the classification is too weak to proceed automatically.

verification_spec.scope_note: Escalate before planning when confidence is below threshold.

cases.expected_rationale: Confidence below 0.6 forces escalate. The change-surface flags do not activate any overlay-driven stage in this deterministic harness.

cases.title: Trace the intermittent worker timeout during receipt rendering
cases.summary: Medium-confidence task that should route through research with a low_confidence label.

classification_evidence: The issue references an intermittent timeout in an existing rendering flow.
The task sounds like a bugfix, but the failing boundary still needs research.

supported.claim: Receipt rendering sometimes times out in the worker path.

supported_claims: A timeout occurs on an existing runtime path.

section_policy.reason: Runtime timeout behavior requires deeper research before planning.
Intermittent failures need explicit risk accounting.

primary_verifier.rationale: Research should confirm the failing path before planning the bugfix.

verification_spec.scope_note: Low-confidence classification should still route through research, not straight to planning.

cases.expected_rationale: Confidence in the middle band routes to low_confidence. Runtime and user-facing flags activate only the observability/operator path in this harness.

cases.title: Refactor retry helper wiring but verifier targets the wrong module
cases.summary: Refactor task with target_files set to a non-overlapping path so Gate 0 must revise for missing verifier coverage.

classification_evidence: The task reorganizes existing retry-helper wiring without adding net-new behavior.
The verifier contract is explicit, but the named target_files do not match the task file list.

supported.claim: Retry helper wiring should move into a shared module.

supported_claims: Shared retry-helper extraction is the scope.

section_policy.reason: The refactor does not change a runtime path or user-facing operation.
Wiring refactors can silently break reuse paths.

primary_verifier.target: unit test: shared retry helper preserves retry budget
primary_verifier.rationale: The refactor still needs a concrete failing verifier.

verification_spec.scope_note: Verifier target_files are intentionally misaligned to exercise the scope check.

cases.expected_rationale: The only new mechanical condition here is verifier scope: target_files points at src/legacy/cli_retry.rs while the task edits src/shared/retry_helper.rs, so Gate 0 must revise with verifier_no_coverage.

cases.title: Add guard assertions to the account CSV export path after QA
cases.summary: Feature task with intersecting verifier target_files and post-QA metrics that fail the mutation threshold.

surviving_mutants.summary: Duplicate-row guard removed from export assertion.

classification_evidence: The task adds stronger export behavior checks after QA surfaced gaps.
The change remains user-facing and runtime-visible.

supported.claim: CSV export needs stronger behavioral coverage.

supported_claims: Export coverage strengthening is the scoped task.

section_policy.reason: CSV export remains a runtime and user-facing operation.
The interface and output expectations remain externally visible.

primary_verifier.target: acceptance test: CSV export preserves expected headers and guard assertions
primary_verifier.rationale: The new guard assertions define the behavior boundary.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case exercises the positive verifier-scope path and the weak test-strength path.

cases.expected_rationale: Coverage clears the 0.80 threshold, but mutation kill rate misses the 0.60 threshold, so test_strength must return weak. target_files intersects the modified export file, so verifier_scope passes.

cases.title: Strengthen invoice retry regression coverage after QA
cases.summary: Bugfix task with intersecting verifier target_files and post-QA metrics that pass both strength thresholds.

surviving_mutants.summary: Equivalent retry log wording change only.

classification_evidence: The task hardens an existing invoice retry bugfix with stronger regression checks.
The bug remains on a runtime, user-visible billing path.

supported.claim: Invoice retry regression coverage needs strengthening.

supported_claims: Invoice retry coverage strengthening is the scoped task.

section_policy.reason: Retry handling remains a runtime and user-facing billing operation.
Billing regressions still need explicit risk accounting.

primary_verifier.target: integration test: invoice retry creates exactly one invoice
primary_verifier.rationale: The regression test should remain the narrowest deterministic signal.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case exercises the passing test-strength path.

cases.expected_rationale: Both post-QA thresholds clear the deterministic gates, so test_strength returns pass. target_files intersects the edited billing file, so verifier_scope also passes.

cases.title: Sketch a vague analytics workspace feature request
cases.summary: Middle-band feature intake that should route through the brief-clarity judge before downstream work proceeds.

classification_evidence: The request points at a new workspace panel but leaves the user flow vague.
The task sounds user-facing, but the success path still needs clarification.

supported.claim: The analytics workspace needs a new user-facing panel.

section_policy.reason: The request affects a runtime and user-facing path.
The vague scope needs explicit risk accounting before planning.

primary_verifier.target: ui test: analytics workspace panel renders the requested flow
primary_verifier.rationale: A user-facing feature should anchor on a concrete UI verifier once clarified.

verification_spec.scope_note: The panel request is intentionally ambiguous enough to stay in the middle confidence band.

cases.expected_rationale: The middle confidence band now triggers brief-clarity judgement instead of treating low_confidence as a purely deterministic route.

cases.title: Audit whether the billing verifier actually checks invoice semantics
cases.summary: High-confidence bugfix with intersecting verifier target files that should also trigger semantic verifier judgement.

classification_evidence: The task tightens an existing billing retry bugfix.
The verifier names the same billing file the task modifies.

supported.claim: Invoice retry semantics still need explicit regression coverage.

section_policy.reason: Retry handling is still a runtime billing path.
The request is defined by deterministic regression verification.

primary_verifier.target: integration test: invoice retry emits one invoice and one audit row
primary_verifier.rationale: The verifier names the semantic billing contract, not just the module path.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case exists to prove that intersecting target_files are only a zero-cost screen.

cases.expected_rationale: Verifier scope intersection now triggers semantic coverage judgement instead of assuming file overlap is enough.

cases.title: Refresh support docs for rotating session tokens
cases.summary: Docs-only task whose deterministic section policy would suppress security review unless the section-policy judge promotes it.

classification_evidence: The task edits runbook content only.
The topic is credential rotation guidance, which carries security meaning despite the docs surface.

supported.claim: The runbook explains how operators rotate support-session tokens.

section_policy.reason: Credential-rotation guidance should still receive explicit security review.
The docs update still needs deterministic lint and link validation.

primary_verifier.rationale: The docs task should still verify the edited runbook deterministically.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case proves that section policy may be overridden by manifest evidence even on docs-only work.

cases.expected_rationale: The deterministic section policy would suppress security review, so the section-policy judge must be eligible to promote it based on manifest evidence.

cases.title: Tighten launch email copy after regex-only slop checks pass
cases.summary: Docs content that clears regex but should still invoke the slop judge before delivery.

classification_evidence: The task edits outbound launch copy.
The work is purely human-facing documentation and messaging.

supported.claim: The launch email copy needs cleanup before sending.

section_policy.reason: The content ships on a user-facing operational path.
Copy changes still need deterministic linting plus content review.

primary_verifier.rationale: The message should still pass deterministic markdown checks.

verification_spec.scope_note: The regex screen is intentionally not enough for this case.

cases.expected_rationale: Content that clears regex still needs slop judgement when the work is human-facing copy.

cases.title: Classify surviving billing mutants for materiality
cases.summary: Post-QA bugfix case where strong aggregate metrics still fail because a surviving mutant is material.

surviving_mutants.summary: The retry dedupe branch can be removed without failing the suite.

classification_evidence: The task hardens retry-window bugfix coverage after QA.
The changed path remains a runtime billing flow.

supported.claim: Surviving billing mutants need classification before sign-off.

supported_claims: Mutation survivors remain on a billing path.

section_policy.reason: Billing retry remains a runtime path.
The case is defined by deterministic test-strength measurement.

primary_verifier.target: integration test: retry window dedupes duplicate invoices
primary_verifier.rationale: The mutation audit should stay anchored to the billing behavior.

verification_spec.scope_note: Material surviving mutants now dominate the verdict.

cases.expected_rationale: A material surviving mutant now forces `weak` even when the aggregate mutation kill rate is still above threshold.

cases.title: Classify trivial surviving scheduler mutants
cases.summary: Post-QA refactor case where surviving mutants are judged trivial and the deterministic thresholds still pass.

surviving_mutants.summary: Equivalent timezone log string survives without changing scheduling behavior.

classification_evidence: The task keeps behavior stable while tightening scheduler coverage.
The surviving mutant is on an internal scheduling helper.

supported.claim: Scheduler mutation survivors need triage after QA.

supported_claims: The task is internal scheduler hardening.

section_policy.reason: Scheduler execution is a runtime path even without direct user-facing behavior.
The case is defined by test-strength verification.

primary_verifier.target: unit test: scheduler next_run preserves current cadence
primary_verifier.rationale: The refactor still needs deterministic cadence verification.

verification_spec.scope_note: Trivial survivors should not force weak when the thresholds otherwise pass.

cases.expected_rationale: The materiality judge still runs, but trivial survivors no longer downgrade an otherwise passing audit.

cases.title: Validate a multi-surface export flow plan
cases.summary: High-confidence feature case that activates both architect and operator overlays while leaving the fast judges available.

classification_evidence: The task adds a new export flow spanning API and runtime job execution.
Both service boundaries and user-facing behavior change.

supported.claim: The export flow spans API, runtime job execution, and a user-visible format change.

supported_claims: The task crosses API and runtime boundaries.

section_policy.reason: The export flow changes a runtime and user-facing path.
The request does not name rollout mechanics.

primary_verifier.target: integration test: export flow returns the new CSV contract
primary_verifier.rationale: The feature is defined by a deterministic export contract.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case exercises the architect plus operator overlay activation path.

cases.expected_rationale: Architect and operator overlays should both activate on a feature that changes API shape, file format, and runtime behavior.

cases.title: Run plan review on a runtime without fast_judge availability
cases.summary: Fast-judge unavailability should surface as a machine-readable judge decision instead of silently falling back to counting rules.

classification_evidence: The task updates operational docs only.
No runtime implementation change is requested.

supported.claim: The incident handoff doc needs revision.

section_policy.reason: The doc still needs deterministic validation.

primary_verifier.rationale: The docs edit should still validate deterministically.

verification_spec.scope_note: This case exists to prove there is no silent counting fallback when fast_judge is missing.

cases.expected_rationale: When fast_judge is unavailable, the judge-invocation policy must record an unavailable decision instead of pretending specificity was measured deterministically.
